seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies structured data extraction from unstructured documents by patching LLM clients to return validated Pydantic models, enabling schema-guided responses from models like GPT-4. However, LLMs can fail with partial extractions, hallucinated enum values, or inconsistent nested structures, leading to unreliable pipelines. Vex provides essential guardrails through verification, ensuring schema compliance and field accuracy for production-grade data extraction.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture begins with document ingestion (PDFs, emails, reports) into a preprocessing layer that chunks text and embeds it for retrieval. Instructor then patches the OpenAI client, enabling schema-guided LLM calls where Pydantic models define the target extraction schema. The LLM generates structured JSON that Instructor validates and retries on failure, returning typed Python objects for downstream processing like database insertion or analytics.

Key components include the Instructor-patched client for extraction, optional vector stores for RAG-enhanced context, and validation loops that handle common LLM inconsistencies. This creates a robust pipeline from raw documents to structured data, but requires verification to catch edge cases like missing required fields or hallucinated values.

Production deployments often layer caching, async processing, and monitoring to scale extraction across thousands of documents while maintaining data quality.

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor's built-in retries fail after max attempts, returning None or partial data when LLM consistently violates schema
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies final output with independent model checks and fallback extraction strategies

risks.name: Partial extraction missing required fields
risks.description: LLM omits critical required fields from Pydantic model, causing validation failure or silent data loss
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces completeness checks across all required schema fields with field presence assertions

risks.name: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
risks.description: LLM generates enum values not present in Pydantic schema (e.g., 'purple' for color enum ['red','blue'])
risks.mitigation: Vex validates enum constraints with exact match verification and correction prompts

risks.name: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
risks.description: LLM creates varying levels of nesting in hierarchical data (addresses, org charts), breaking downstream processing
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces consistent nesting depth and structure through recursive schema validation

implementation.title: Basic Instructor Pydantic Extraction
implementation.description: Patch OpenAI client and extract simple invoice data using Pydantic model validation
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import Optional

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(..., description="Unique invoice ID")
    total_amount: float
    date: str
    customer: str

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    response_model=Invoice,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Extract invoice data: Invoice #INV-1234 dated 2024-10-01 for Acme Corp total $2,500"}
    ]
)

print(response.model_dump_json(indent=2))

implementation.title: Nested Document Extraction
implementation.description: Extract hierarchical data with nested Pydantic models for complex documents
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List
from openai import OpenAI

class Address(BaseModel):
    street: str
    city: str
    zip_code: str

class Employee(BaseModel):
    name: str
    role: str
    address: Address

class OrgChart(BaseModel):
    company: str
    employees: List[Employee]

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

org_data = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=OrgChart,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract org chart: Acme Corp - CEO John Doe at 123 Main St, SF 94105; CTO Jane Smith at 456 Oak Ave, SF 94103"}]
)

print(org_data.model_dump_json(indent=2))

implementation.description: TypeScript equivalent using Zod schemas for structured extraction

implementation.title: Enum-Constrained Extraction
implementation.description: Extract with strict enum validation to prevent hallucinated values
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum
from openai import OpenAI

class DocumentType(str, Enum):
    INVOICE = "invoice"
    RECEIPT = "receipt"
    CONTRACT = "contract"

class Document(BaseModel):
    doc_type: DocumentType
    amount: float = Field(..., ge=0)
    vendor: str

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

doc = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    response_model=Document,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Extract: This invoice from VendorX for $1500"}]
)

print(f"Type: {doc.doc_type}, Amount: ${doc.amount}")

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Instructor with Vex Schema Verification
vexIntegration.description: Protect against validation exhaustion by verifying final Pydantic output with Vex
vexIntegration.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from vex import verify, Verifier
from openai import OpenAI

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str
    total_amount: float
    date: str

# Vex verifier for schema compliance
invoice_verifier = Verifier(
    checks=[
        "schema_compliance",
        "field_accuracy",
        "completeness_validation"
    ]
)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

@verify(verifier=invoice_verifier)
def extract_invoice(text: str) -> Invoice:
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        response_model=Invoice,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": text}]
    )

# Safe extraction
invoice = extract_invoice("Invoice #123 total $2500 on 2024-10-01")
print(invoice.model_dump_json())

vexIntegration.title: Vex Nested Structure Consistency Check
vexIntegration.description: Ensure consistent nesting depth for hierarchical extractions
vexIntegration.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List
from vex import verify, Verifier
from openai import OpenAI

class Address(BaseModel):
    street: str
    city: str

class OrgChart(BaseModel):
    company: str
    departments: List[dict]  # Dynamic nesting

org_verifier = Verifier(
    checks=[
        "schema_compliance",
        "type_consistency",
        "nested_structure_consistency"
    ]
)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

@verify(verifier=org_verifier)
def extract_org_chart(text: str) -> OrgChart:
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=OrgChart,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": text}]
    )

org = extract_org_chart("Acme Corp: Engineering (SF), Sales (NYC)")
print(org.model_dump_json(indent=2))
